content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 17, 2004
Derek Johnson, General Manager
Isla Vista Recreation & Park District
961 Embarcadero Del Mar
Isla Vista, CA 93117

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-04-244

Dear Mr. Johnson:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Boardmember Bryan Brown for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Since your request largely seeks general guidance, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.²

There is nothing in the Act that prohibits a public official such as Boardmember Brown, from holding two public positions concurrently, although other laws outside the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission ("Commission") may restrict the ability of a public official from holding two public offices simultaneously, if those offices are determined to be "incompatible" offices. You are urged to check with the state Attorney General's office or the city attorney's office of your jurisdiction to determine if there are other laws that may be applicable in light of the facts you present. Holding positions on both the Isla Vista Recreation and Parks District ("IVRPD") and the project area committee ("PAC")/general plan advisory committee ("GPAC") does not, in itself, create a potential disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. (See e.g. Foster Advice Letter, No. A-02-125, copy enclosed.) The Commission's advice is limited to matters arising under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(d).) The information you provided with your request for advice pertains to advice you received from the Santa Barbara County Counsel regarding Government Code 1090 issues. The Commission does not provide advice on Government Code 1090 and again, we recommend you contact the state Attorney General's office for further information and guidance.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), enclosed.)

File No. 04-244
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By:[signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

GW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\I-04-244

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a public official can hold two concurrent positions (on the Isla Vista Recreation and Parks District and a project area committee/general plan advisory committee) and whether such positions create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a public official from holding two public positions concurrently, and the specific positions described do not create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. However, other state laws regarding incompatible offices may apply, and Government Code 1090 issues should be directed to the State Attorney General's office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers present; content extracted from body of informal advice letter. Document clearly marked as informal assistance, which does not provide immunity. References enclosed Foster Advice Letter No. 4-02-125 for precedent.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a public official can hold two concurrent positions (on the Isla Vista Recreation and Parks District and a project area committee/general plan advisory committee) and whether such positions create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not prohibit a public official from holding two public positions concurrently, and the specific positions described do not create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act. However, other state laws regarding incompatible offices may apply, and Government Code 1090 issues should be directed to the State Attorney General's office.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provided informal advice to the Isla Vista Recreation & Park District regarding whether Boardmember Bryan Brown can hold two concurrent public positions without creating a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC concluded that the Act itself permits concurrent positions and the specific circumstances presented do not create a disqualifying conflict, though other applicable laws may restrict such positions.
